name: HMS ML 460 (ML 460)
events: 9 Jun 1943 In a signal timed 0938 B/9, the Capt. S.8 sent the following orders / information to HrMs Dolfijn (Lt.Cdr. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) and HMS Seraph (Lt. N.L.A. Jewell, OBE, RN); Your rendezvous tomorrow will be in position 37°00'N, 03°16'E at 0600 B/10th.

In a signal timed 1414 B/9, the Capt. S.8 informed the same submarines of the following; 1) HMS Coriolanus (T/Lt. N. Hunt, RNVR) and HMML 460 (T/S.Lt. K.D. Dewar, RNVR) will meet you in position 37°00'N, 03°16'E at 0600 B/10. The first arrival will be escorted to harbour by ML 460, the second by Coriolanus. 2) A large eastbound convoy will be passing Algiers around 0700 B/10. 3) ML's are patrolling east and west of Algiers. (1)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
